_meta.description: Octoechos — variable hymns organized by tone and day. The 8-tone cycle governs which tone's content is used on a given week.
_meta.note: Populated incrementally from real OCA service examples. Missing hymns are silently skipped by the assembler.
_meta.version: 0.2.0 — Tone 3 dogmatikon; Tone 4 full Saturday Vespers; Tone 8 partial Saturday Vespers

sources.tone3.saturday.vespers.dogmatikon: OCA service texts 2025-11-01 (Holy Unmercenaries Cosmas & Damian, Polyeleos)
sources.tone4.saturday.vespers.*: OCA service texts 2026-02-22 (Forgiveness Sunday — note: Sunday Vigil format, some Saturday-specific stichera still TODO)
sources.tone8.saturday.vespers.*: OCA service texts 2026-04-18 (Bright Saturday — note: Paschal season context)

tone3.note: Only the Dogmatikon has been populated from a real service (Nov 1, 2025). All other Saturday Vespers content is TODO.

dogmatikon.label: Theotokion — Dogmatikon
dogmatikon.text:
How can we not wonder
at thy mystical childbearing, O exalted Mother?
For without receiving the touch of man,
thou gavest birth to a Son in the flesh, O immaculate Virgin.
The Son born of the Father before eternity
was born of thee at the fullness of time, O honored Lady.
He underwent no mingling, no change, no division,
but preserved the fullness of each nature.
Entreat Him, O Lady and Virgin and Mother,
to save the souls of those who, in the Orthodox manner, confess thee
to be the Theotokos.

dogmatikon.label: Theotokion — Dogmatikon
dogmatikon.text:
The Prophet David was a father of the Lord through thee, O Virgin.
He foretold in songs the One Who worked wonder in thee:
"At Thy right hand stood the Queen,"
Thy Mother, the mediatrix of life,
since God was freely born of her without a father.
He wanted to renew His fallen image, made corrupt in passion,
so He took the lost sheep upon His shoulder
and brought it to His Father,
joining it to the heavenly pow'rs.
Christ, Who has great and rich mercy, has saved the world, O Theotokos.

lordICall.note: Hymns 1–4 extracted from the Sunday Vigil (Forgiveness Sunday, Feb 22, 2026). Hymns 5–6 (verses 6 and 5 on a regular Saturday) were displaced by Triodion stichera on that occasion and remain TODO.

hymns.note: verse 10 on a Sunday Vigil; would be verse 6 on a regular Saturday with 6 resurrectional stichera
hymns.text:
We glorify Thy Resurrection on the third day, O Christ God,
by always honoring Thy life-creating Cross;
by it, Thou hast renewed the corrupted nature of man, O almighty One.
By it, Thou hast renewed our entrance to heaven,
for Thou art good and the Lover of man.

Thou didst loose the Tree's verdict of disobedience, O Savior,
by being voluntarily nailed to the tree of the Cross.
By descending to hell, O almighty God,
Thou didst break the bonds of death.
Therefore, we adore Thy Resurrection from the dead, singing in joy:
"Glory to Thee, O all-powerful Lord!"

Thou didst smash the gates of hell, O Lord,
and by Thy death Thou didst demolish the kingdom of death.
Thou didst deliver the race of men from corruption,
granting the world life, incorruption and great mercy.

Come, O people,
let us sing of the Savior's Resurrection on the third day!
By it, we have been freed from the unbreakable bonds of hell.
By it, we have received life and incorruption.
Save us by Thy Resurrection!
O Life-creating and almighty Savior, glory to Thee!

hymns.note: TODO — verse 6 of regular Saturday Lord I Call
TODO — verse 5 of regular Saturday Lord I Call

aposticha.note: These are Sunday Vigil aposticha (Ps. 92 verses). Saturday aposticha use different psalm verses (from fixedTexts.aposticha.saturdayVerses) and the stichera themselves may also differ. Treat these as approximate until a plain Saturday in Tone 4 is found.

hymns.text:
By ascending the Cross, O Lord,
Thou hast annulled our ancestral curse.
By descending to hell, Thou hast freed the eternal prisoners,
granting incorruption to the human race.
Therefore in songs we glorify
Thy life-creating and saving Resurrection.

By hanging upon the tree, O only powerful Lord,
Thou didst shake all of creation.
By being laid in the tomb
Thou hast raised those who dwelt in the tombs,
granting life and incorruption to the human race.
Therefore in songs we glorify Thy rising on the third day.

The lawless people, O Christ,
handed Thee over to Pilate, condemned to be crucified,
thus proving themselves ungrateful before their Benefactor.
But voluntarily Thou didst endure burial,
rising by Thine own power on the third day as God,
granting us life everlasting and great mercy.

With tears the women reached Thy tomb,
searching for Thee, but not finding Thee.
They wept with wailing and lamented:
"Woe to us! Our Savior, King of all,
how wast Thou stolen?
What place can hold Thy life-bearing body?"
An Angel replied to them:
"Do not weep, but go and proclaim
that the Lord is risen,
granting us joy as the only compassionate One!"

theotokion.note: TODO — Saturday aposticha theotokion for Tone 4 to be populated

troparion.label: Resurrectional Troparion
troparion.text:
When the women disciples of the Lord
learned from the angel the joyous message of Thy Resurrection,
they cast away the ancestral curse
and elatedly told the apostles:
"Death is overthrown!
Christ God is risen,
granting the world great mercy!"

dismissalTheotokion.label: Resurrectional Dismissal Theotokion
dismissalTheotokion.text:
The mystery of all eternity, unknown even by Angels,
through thee, O Theotokos, is revealed to those on earth:
God incarnate by union without confusion.
He voluntarily accepted the Cross for us,
by which He resurrected the first-created man,
saving our souls from death.

dogmatikon.label: Theotokion — Dogmatikon
dogmatikon.note: Always used as the 'Now and ever' at Lord I Call on Saturday Great Vespers when Tone 5 is the weekly tone
dogmatikon.text:
In the Red Sea of old,
a type of the Virgin Bride was prefigured.
There Moses divided the waters;
here Gabriel assisted in the miracle.
There Israel crossed the sea without getting wet,
here the Virgin gave birth to Christ without seed.
After Israel's passage, the sea remained impassable;
after Emmanuel's birth, the Virgin remained a Virgin.
O ever-existing God Who didst appear as Man,
O Lord, have mercy on us!

lordICall.note: On Soul Saturdays during Lent, the resurrectional stichera are replaced by Triodion martyria and departed texts. Dogmatikon remains from Octoechos.

tone8.note: Extracted from OCA Bright Saturday Vespers (Apr 18, 2026). All 6 Lord I Call stichera and Dogmatikon are present. Aposticha are partial (Paschal stichera replaced positions 2–4 on that occasion).

dogmatikon.label: Theotokion — Dogmatikon
dogmatikon.text:
The King of heaven, because of His love for man,
appeared on earth and dwelt with men.
He took flesh from the pure Virgin
and after assuming it, He came forth from her.
The Son is one: in two natures, yet one Person.
Proclaiming Him as perfect God and perfect Man,
we confess Christ our God!
Entreat Him, O unwedded Mother, to have mercy on our souls.

resurrectional.note: All 6 stichera extracted from Bright Saturday Vespers (Apr 18, 2026). On a regular Saturday with 6 resurrectional stichera these fill verses 6–1.

hymns.text:
We offer to Thee, O Christ,
our evening song and reasonable service,
for Thou didst will to have mercy on us
by Thy Resurrection.

Lord, Lord, do not cast us away from Thy face!
For Thou didst will to have mercy on us
by Thy Resurrection.

Rejoice, O holy Zion:
Mother of the churches, the abode of God,
for thou wast the first to receive remission of sins
by the Resurrection.

The Word of God the Father,
begotten before the ages,
in the latter times willed to be incarnate of the Virgin
and endured crucifixion unto death.
He has saved mortal man
by His Resurrection.

We glorify Thee, O Lord,
for Thou didst voluntarily endure the Cross for our sake.
We fall down in worship before Thee, O almighty Savior.
Do not cast us away from Thy face
but hear us and save us, O Lover of man,
by Thy Resurrection!

Glory to Thee, O Savior Christ,
Only-begotten Son of God,
who wast nailed to the Cross
and resurrected from the tomb on the third day!

glory.text:
We glorify Thy Resurrection from the dead, O Christ,
by which Thou hast freed Adam's race from the tortures of hell,
and as God hast granted to the world eternal life and great mercy.

aposticha.note: Only the first sticheron (idiomelon) is from a clean Tone 8 source. Positions 2–3 need a plain Saturday in Tone 8.

hymns.label: Idiomelon
hymns.text:
Descending from heaven to ascend the Cross,
the eternal Life has come for death
to raise those who are fallen;
to enlighten those in darkness.
O Jesus, our Savior and illuminator, glory to Thee!
